vulnerabilities.name: Prompt Injection
vulnerabilities.description: Attacker crafts inputs to manipulate LLM behavior by overriding system prompts or hijacking model instructions.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Implement prompt hardening, input validation, output filtering, and privilege separation between system/user contexts.

vulnerabilities.name: Direct Prompt Injection
vulnerabilities.description: Attacker directly injects malicious instructions into user-visible prompt fields to override system behavior.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Validate and sanitize user inputs; enforce strict separation between instructions and data.

vulnerabilities.name: Indirect Prompt Injection
vulnerabilities.description: Malicious instructions embedded in external content (web pages, documents) retrieved by the LLM at runtime.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Sanitize all external content before processing; mark content source contexts; apply content trust levels.

vulnerabilities.name: Prompt Jailbreaking
vulnerabilities.description: Adversarial prompts bypass safety guardrails and alignment policies of the LLM.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Implement multi-layered safety filters, red-teaming, and adversarial training. Monitor jailbreak attempts.

vulnerabilities.name: Instruction Hijacking via Retrieved Content
vulnerabilities.description: Malicious content in retrieved data overrides original instructions causing unintended model actions.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Enforce context isolation; validate retrieved content; use output validation layers.

vulnerabilities.name: Sensitive Information Disclosure via LLM
vulnerabilities.description: LLM inadvertently reveals sensitive information including PII, credentials, or proprietary data in responses.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Implement output filtering, data minimization in training, and access controls on model deployment.

vulnerabilities.name: Training Data Leakage / Memorization
vulnerabilities.description: LLM memorizes and reproduces sensitive training data (PII, credentials, trade secrets) during inference.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply differential privacy, data sanitization pre-training, and output monitoring for sensitive data patterns.

vulnerabilities.name: PII Exposure in LLM Responses
vulnerabilities.description: Personally Identifiable Information (PII) is exposed through LLM responses due to memorization or permissive queries.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Implement PII detection/redaction in outputs; enforce data minimization; conduct privacy impact assessments.

vulnerabilities.name: Confidential Data Extraction via Prompting
vulnerabilities.description: Attacker crafts queries to extract confidential information stored in the LLM context or training data.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply query rate limiting, output filtering, and context window isolation.

vulnerabilities.name: Privacy Violation via LLM Processing
vulnerabilities.description: LLM processing of personal data leads to privacy violations through unintended disclosure or aggregation.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Conduct DPIAs, apply data minimization, implement consent management, and audit LLM data flows.

vulnerabilities.name: Malicious Model Dependencies
vulnerabilities.description: Compromised third-party libraries or tools used in LLM pipeline introduce malicious functionality.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Enforce SBOM, verify package integrity, use dependency scanning tools.

vulnerabilities.name: Compromised Pre-trained Models
vulnerabilities.description: Pre-trained model weights from untrusted sources contain backdoors or malicious modifications.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Only use models from verified sources; validate model checksums; implement model provenance tracking.

vulnerabilities.name: Vulnerable Third-Party LLM Integrations
vulnerabilities.description: Third-party LLM plugins, APIs, or SDKs contain known vulnerabilities exploitable in the LLM pipeline.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Inventory all third-party components; apply regular patching; use isolated integration environments.

vulnerabilities.name: Malicious Plugin / Extension for LLM
vulnerabilities.description: Untrusted or compromised LLM plugins execute malicious code or exfiltrate data during operation.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Implement plugin sandboxing, code signing, and least-privilege execution for all LLM extensions.

vulnerabilities.name: Tainted Model Weights
vulnerabilities.description: Model weights are tampered post-training to introduce hidden behaviors or backdoor triggers.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Cryptographically sign model artifacts; verify integrity at load time; maintain secure model registry.

vulnerabilities.name: Training Data Poisoning
vulnerabilities.description: Attacker injects malicious samples into training datasets to introduce biases, backdoors, or unsafe behaviors.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Validate training data sources; implement anomaly detection on datasets; use data provenance tracking.

vulnerabilities.name: Fine-tuning Data Manipulation
vulnerabilities.description: Malicious data injected during fine-tuning phase to override safety alignment or implant backdoors.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Audit fine-tuning datasets; restrict fine-tuning access; monitor model behavior changes post fine-tuning.

vulnerabilities.name: RAG Data Poisoning
vulnerabilities.description: Attacker contaminates the RAG knowledge base to influence LLM responses with false or harmful content.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Validate and sanitize RAG data sources; implement access controls on vector databases; monitor retrieval patterns.

vulnerabilities.name: Backdoor Attacks on LLM
vulnerabilities.description: Hidden triggers embedded in training data cause the model to behave maliciously on specific inputs.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Conduct adversarial testing; implement runtime behavior monitoring; use interpretability tools.

vulnerabilities.name: Adversarial Input Attacks
vulnerabilities.description: Carefully crafted inputs cause the LLM to misclassify, malfunction, or produce harmful outputs.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Implement adversarial training, input preprocessing, and anomaly detection on model inputs.

vulnerabilities.name: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) via LLM Output
vulnerabilities.description: LLM-generated content containing malicious scripts rendered in a browser without sanitization leads to XSS.
vulnerabilities.remediation: HTML-encode all LLM outputs rendered in browsers; implement CSP; sanitize before rendering.

vulnerabilities.name: Code Injection via LLM Output
vulnerabilities.description: Unvalidated LLM-generated code executed directly allows arbitrary code execution.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Never execute LLM output directly; validate and sandbox all generated code before execution.

vulnerabilities.name: SQL Injection via LLM-Generated Query
vulnerabilities.description: LLM-generated SQL queries containing malicious content executed against databases.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Use parameterized queries; validate LLM SQL output; apply database least privilege.

vulnerabilities.name: Path Traversal via LLM-Generated Path
vulnerabilities.description: LLM generates file paths with traversal sequences that allow access to unauthorized files.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Validate and canonicalize all LLM-generated file paths; use allowlists for permitted directories.

vulnerabilities.name: Unvalidated LLM Output Execution
vulnerabilities.description: Direct execution or rendering of LLM output without validation allows injection into downstream components.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Implement output validation layers; treat LLM output as untrusted input to downstream systems.

vulnerabilities.name: LLM-Enabled Server-Side Injection
vulnerabilities.description: LLM output fed into server-side interpreters (shell, template engines) without sanitization.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Escape and validate all LLM outputs before use in interpreters; use safe API equivalents.

vulnerabilities.name: Excessive Permissions for LLM Agents
vulnerabilities.description: LLM agents granted permissions beyond what is necessary for tasks, enabling broader impact from compromised outputs.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply least privilege principles; audit agent permissions; restrict actions to minimum necessary scope.

vulnerabilities.name: Unnecessary LLM Functionality / Feature Creep
vulnerabilities.description: LLM system includes unnecessary capabilities or API access that expands attack surface.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Minimize LLM capabilities; disable unused integrations; regularly audit what the agent can do.

vulnerabilities.name: Lack of Human Oversight for LLM Actions
vulnerabilities.description: LLM agents take consequential real-world actions without human review or approval mechanisms.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Implement human-in-the-loop checkpoints for high-risk actions; require confirmation for destructive operations.

vulnerabilities.name: Autonomous Action Without Approval
vulnerabilities.description: LLM autonomously executes sensitive operations (send emails, delete files) without user consent.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Require explicit user approval for all sensitive actions; implement action audit trails.

vulnerabilities.name: Over-privileged LLM Plugin / Tool
vulnerabilities.description: LLM tool plugins with excessive permissions allow broad unintended system access or data exposure.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Scope plugin permissions tightly; review and approve each plugin capability; use sandboxed execution.

vulnerabilities.name: System Prompt Extraction
vulnerabilities.description: Attacker crafts queries to extract confidential system prompt instructions from the LLM.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Implement prompt hardening; do not rely on system prompt secrecy for security; use access control layers.

vulnerabilities.name: Confidential Instruction Disclosure via Prompt
vulnerabilities.description: Sensitive business logic or credentials embedded in system prompts are disclosed through adversarial queries.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Never store sensitive data in system prompts; use external secure storage; monitor prompt extraction attempts.

vulnerabilities.name: Prompt Leakage via Side-Channels
vulnerabilities.description: System prompt content inferred through timing, token counts, or response patterns without direct extraction.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Normalize response timing; avoid information leakage through metadata; implement output rate controls.

vulnerabilities.name: Meta-Prompt / Configuration Disclosure
vulnerabilities.description: Internal LLM configuration, persona instructions, or tool descriptions inadvertently leaked in responses.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Sanitize model responses for internal metadata; test for configuration disclosure in all output paths.

vulnerabilities.name: Embedding Inversion Attack
vulnerabilities.description: Attacker reconstructs original sensitive text from embedding vectors stored in vector databases.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Apply differential privacy to embeddings; restrict direct access to embedding stores; encrypt at rest.

vulnerabilities.name: RAG Poisoning via Vector Store
vulnerabilities.description: Malicious documents injected into vector database cause RAG-based LLM to return harmful content.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Validate all documents before indexing; implement content moderation on vector store inputs.

vulnerabilities.name: Cross-Context Information Leakage via Embeddings
vulnerabilities.description: Semantic similarity search exposes documents from different security contexts or tenants.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Implement tenant isolation in vector databases; enforce access control at retrieval level.

vulnerabilities.name: Semantic Similarity Attack
vulnerabilities.description: Attacker uses semantically similar queries to bypass content filters or retrieve restricted information.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Implement semantic-aware content filtering; use adversarial robustness techniques on retrieval systems.

vulnerabilities.name: Vector Database Injection
vulnerabilities.description: Malicious metadata or filter parameters in vector database queries cause unintended data access.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Sanitize all vector DB query parameters; use parameterized queries; apply input validation.

vulnerabilities.name: LLM Hallucination / False Information Generation
vulnerabilities.description: LLM generates plausible but factually incorrect information presented with false confidence.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Implement RAG with verified sources; add fact-checking layers; clearly communicate uncertainty to users.

vulnerabilities.name: Overreliance on LLM Output
vulnerabilities.description: Users or systems treat LLM outputs as authoritative without verification, leading to harmful decisions.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Display confidence indicators; require human verification for critical decisions; educate users on LLM limitations.

vulnerabilities.name: Disinformation Generation via LLM
vulnerabilities.description: LLM used to generate large-scale disinformation, fake news, or manipulated content.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Implement usage policies; detect and block harmful content generation; apply watermarking to AI-generated content.

vulnerabilities.name: Fabricated Citations and References
vulnerabilities.description: LLM generates fake academic citations, legal references, or URLs that appear legitimate.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Validate all citations against real sources; use grounding techniques with verified knowledge bases.

vulnerabilities.name: AI-Generated Deepfake Content Enablement
vulnerabilities.description: LLM used to generate or assist in creating synthetic identity content for fraud or manipulation.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Implement content provenance tracking; apply usage restrictions; use AI content detection tools.

vulnerabilities.name: Denial of Service (DoS) via LLM
vulnerabilities.description: Attacker submits computationally expensive prompts to exhaust model resources and deny service to legitimate users.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Implement rate limiting per user/IP; set maximum token limits; use request queuing and timeout mechanisms.

vulnerabilities.name: Resource Exhaustion via Token Flooding
vulnerabilities.description: Massive token requests overwhelm LLM inference infrastructure causing service degradation.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Enforce token limits per request; implement cost-based throttling; monitor unusual consumption.

vulnerabilities.name: Model Extraction Attack
vulnerabilities.description: Attacker makes extensive queries to reverse-engineer model weights, architecture, or training data.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Rate limit API queries; detect extraction patterns; apply output perturbation; monitor systematic querying.

vulnerabilities.name: Excessive LLM API Consumption
vulnerabilities.description: Uncontrolled API usage leads to excessive costs, degraded performance, or resource starvation.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Implement usage quotas, budgets, and alerts; enforce per-user billing limits; monitor anomalous consumption.

vulnerabilities.name: Sponge Attack – Energy / Latency Exhaustion
vulnerabilities.description: Adversarially crafted inputs maximize computational requirements causing latency spikes and energy waste.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Deploy adversarial robustness defenses; monitor latency outliers; implement request complexity scoring.
